html:
Act II

The same room, during the evening performance. Occasional music, laughter, shrieks, and applause are audible. Through the small windows, back centre, the light is shining.

Consuelo and Baron Regnard occupy the stage; Consuelo wears her stage costume; she sits with her feet on the sofa, a small shawl covering her shoulders. Before her stands the Baron, a tall stout man in evening dress, a rose in his buttonhole; grasping the ground with feet well apart, he gazes at her with convex spider-like eyes.

Baron Is it true that your father, the Count, has introduced you to a certain Marquis Justi, a very rich man?
Consuelo Surprised. No, he is only joking. I have often heard him speak of a Marquis Justi but I have never seen himвБ†вАФ
Baron And do you know that your father is just a charlatan?
Consuelo Oh! DonвАЩt say thatвБ†вАФFather is such a dear.
Baron Did you like the jewels?
Consuelo Yes, very much. I was very sorry when Father told me I must return them. He said it would not be nice for me to keep them. I even cried a little about it.
Baron Your father is only a beggar and a charlatan.
Consuelo Oh, no, donвАЩt scold himвБ†вАФhe loves you so much.
Baron Let me kiss your handвБ†вАФ
Consuelo Oh, no, it isnвАЩt proper! One may kiss the hand only when one says how do you do or goodbye. But in the meantime you canвАЩt.
Baron Everybody is in love with you, that is why you and your father make such a fuss about yourselves. Who is that new clown they call He? I donвАЩt like him, heвАЩs too shrewd a beast.вБ†вАКвБ†вА¶ Is he in love with you, too? I noticed the way he looked at you.вБ†вАКвБ†вА¶
Consuelo Laughing. Nothing of the kind. He is so funny! He got fifty-two slaps yesterday. We counted them. Think of it, fifty-two slaps! Father said, вАЬif they had only been gold pieces.вАЭ
Baron And Bezano, Consuelo.вБ†вАКвБ†вА¶ Do you like him?
Consuelo Yes, very much. He is so good-looking. He says that Bezano and I are the most beautiful couple in the world. He calls him Adam, and me Eve. But thatвАЩs improper, isnвАЩt it? He is so improper.
Baron And does He speak to you very often?
Consuelo Yes, often.вБ†вАКвБ†вА¶ But I donвАЩt understand him. It seems as if he were drunk.
Baron вАЬConsueloвАЭ!вБ†вАКвБ†вА¶ It means in SpanishвБ†вАКвБ†вА¶ Consolation. Your father is an ass.вБ†вАКвБ†вА¶ Consuelo, I love you.
Consuelo Talk it over with Father.
Baron Angry. Your father is a swindler and a charlatan. He should be turned over to the police. DonвАЩt you understand that I cannot marry you?
Consuelo But Father says you can.вБ†вАКвБ†вА¶
Baron No, I cannot. And what if I shoot myself? Consuelo, silly girl, I love you unbearablyвБ†вАКвБ†вА¶ unbearably, do you understand? I am probably madвБ†вАКвБ†вА¶ and must be taken to a doctor, yanked about, beaten with sticks. Why do I love you so much, Consuelo?
Consuelo Then, youвАЩd better marry.
Baron I have had a hundred women, beauties, but I didnвАЩt see them. You are the first and I donвАЩt see anyone else. Who strikes man with love, God or the Devil? The Devil struck me. Let me kiss your hand.
Consuelo No. She thinks a while and sighs.
Baron Do you think sometimes? What are you thinking about now Consuelo?
Consuelo With another sigh. I donвАЩt know why, I just felt sorry for Bezano. Sighs again. He is so nice to me when he teaches meвБ†вАКвБ†вА¶ and he has such a tiny little room.
Baron Indignant. You were there?
Consuelo No. He told me about it. Smiling. Do you hear the noise in there? ThatвАЩs He getting slapped. Poor thingвБ†вАКвБ†вА¶ although I know it doesnвАЩt hurt, itвАЩs only make-believe. The intermission is coming soon.
The Baron throws away his cigar, takes two quick steps forward, and falls on his knees before the girl.
Baron ConsueloвБ†вАФ
Consuelo Please, donвАЩt. Get up. Please leave my hand alone.
Baron Consuelo!
Consuelo Disgusted. Get up please, itвАЩs disgustingвБ†вАФyouвАЩre so fat.
The Baron gets up. Voices are heard near the door and in the ring. It is the intermission. The clowns come first, talking cheerfully and excitedly. He leads them, in his clownвАЩs dress, with painted eyebrows and white nose; the others are applauding him. Voices of the actors calling: вАЬBravo! He.вАЭ Then come the actors and actresses, riding-masters, and the rest, all in costume. Zinida is not among them. Papa Briquet comes a little later.
Polly A hundred slaps! Bravo, He!
Jackson Not bad, not bad at all. YouвАЩll make a career.
Tilly He was the Professor today, and we were the students. Here goes another! Gives him a clownвАЩs slap. Laughter. All bid good evening to the Baron. He is politely rude to these vagabonds who bore him, and remains silent. They seem quite used to it. Enter Mancini. He is the same, and with the same cane.
Mancini Shaking hands. What a success, BaronвБ†вАФand think of itвБ†вАФhow the crowd does love slaps. Whispering. Your knees are dusty, Baron, brush them off. The floor is very dirty in here. Aloud. Consuelo, dear child, how do you feel? Goes over to his daughter. Sound of laughing, chattering. The waiters from the buffet in the lobby bring in soda and wine. ConsueloвАЩs voice is heard.
Consuelo And where is Bezano?
He Bows before the Baron, affecting intimacy. You do not recognize me, Baron?
Baron Yes I do. You are the clown, He.
He Yes I am He Who Gets Slapped. May I presume to ask you, Baron, did you get your jewels back?
Baron What!
He I was asked to return some jewels to you, and I take the liberty ofвБ†вАФThe Baron turns his back on himвБ†вАФHe laughs loudly.
Jackson Whiskey and soda! Believe me, ladies and gents, He will surely make a career. I am an old clown, and I know the crowd. Why today, he even eclipsed meвБ†вАФand clouds have covered my Sun. Striking it. They do not like puzzles, they want slaps! They are longing for them and dreaming about them in their homes. Your health, He! Another whiskey and soda! He got so many slaps today, there would be enough to go round the whole orchestra!
Tilly I bet there wouldnвАЩt! To Jackson. Shake!
Polly I bet there wouldnвАЩtвБ†вАФIвАЩll go and count the old mugs.
A Voice The orchestra did not laughвБ†вАФ
Jackson Because they were getting it, but the galleries did, because they were looking at the orchestra getting slapped. Your health, He!
He YourвАЩs Jim! Tell me, why didnвАЩt you let me finish my speechвБ†вАФI was just getting a good start.
Jackson Seriously. My friend, because your speech was a sacrilege. PoliticsвБ†вАФall right. MannersвБ†вАФas much as you want. But ProvidenceвБ†вАФleave it in peace. And believe me, friend, I shut your mouth in time. DidnвАЩt I, Papa Briquet?
Briquet Coming nearer. Yes. It was too much like literature. This is not an academy. You forget yourself, He.
Tilly But to shut oneвАЩs mouthвБ†вАФfaugh.вБ†вАКвБ†вА¶
Briquet In a didactic tone. Whenever one shuts oneвАЩs mouth, it is always high time to shut it, unless one is drinking. Hey, whiskey and soda!
Voices Whiskey and soda for the Manager!
Mancini But this is obscurantism. Philosophizing again, Briquet?
Briquet I am not satisfied with you today, He. Why do you tease them? They donвАЩt like it. Your health! A good slap must be clean like a crystalвБ†вАФfft-fft! right side, left side, and done with it. They will like it; they will laugh, and love you. But in your slaps there is a certain bite, you understand, a certain smellвБ†вАФ
He But they laughed, nevertheless!
Briquet But without pleasure, without pleasure, He. You pay, and immediately draw a draft on their bank; itвАЩs not the right gameвБ†вАФthey wonвАЩt like you.
Jackson ThatвАЩs what I tell him. He had already begun to make them angry.
Bezano Entering. Consuelo, where are you? I have been looking for youвБ†вАФcome on. Both go out. The Baron, after hesitating a while, follows them. Mancini accompanies him respectfully to the door.
He Sighs. You donвАЩt understand, my dear friends; you are simply old, and have forgotten the smell of the stage.
Jackson Aha! Who is old, my young man?
He DonвАЩt be angry, Jim. ItвАЩs a play, donвАЩt you understand? I become happy when I enter the ring and hear the music. I wear a mask and I feel humorous. There is a mask on my face, and I play. I may say anything like a drunkard. Do you understand? Yesterday when I, with this stupid face, was playing the great man, the philosopher he assumes a proud monumental pose, and repeats the gesture of the playвБ†вАФgeneral laughter I was walking this way, and was telling how great, how wise, how incomparable I wasвБ†вАФhow God lived in me, how high I stood above the earthвБ†вАФhow glory shone above my head his voice changes and he is speaking faster then you, Jim, you hit me for the first time. And I asked you, вАЬWhat is it, theyвАЩre applauding me?вАЭ Then, at the tenth slap, I said: вАЬIt seems to me that they sent for me from the Academy?вАЭ Acts, looking around him with an air of unconquerable pride and splendour. Laughter. Jackson gives him a real slap.
He Holding his face. Why?
Jackson Because youвАЩre a fool, and play for nothing. Waiter, the check. Laughter. The bell calls them to the ring. The actors go out in haste, some running. The waiters collect their money.
Briquet In a singsong. To the ringвБ†вАФto the ringвБ†вАФ
Mancini I want to tell you something, He. You are not going yet?
He No. IвАЩll take a rest.
Briquet To the ringвБ†вАФto the ringвБ†вАФ
The clowns as they go sing in shrill, squeaky voices. Little by little they all disappear, and loud music begins. He seats himself on the sofa with his legs crossed, and yawns.
Mancini He, you have something none of my ancestors ever hadвБ†вАФmoney. LetвАЩs have a nice bottle on you. Waiter, pleaseвБ†вАФThe waiter who was taking up dishes, brings a bottle of wine and glasses and goes out.
He YouвАЩre blue, Mancini. Stretches. Well, at my age, a hundred slapsвБ†вАФit seems pretty hard. So youвАЩre blue. How are things getting on with your girl?
Mancini Tss! Bad! ComplicationsвБ†вАФparentsвБ†вАФshudders AghвБ†вАФ
He Prison!
Mancini Laughing. Prison! MustnвАЩt I uphold the glory of my name now, eh? He, IвАЩm jokingвБ†вАФbut there is Hell in my heart. YouвАЩre the only one who understands me. But tell me how to explain this passion? It will turn my hair grey, itвАЩll bring me to prison, to the grave. I am a tragic man. HeвБ†вАФWipes his eyes with a dirty handkerchief. Why donвАЩt I like things which are not forbidden? Why, at all moments, even at the very moment of ecstasy, must I be reminded of some lawвБ†вАФit is stupid. He, I am becoming an anarchist. Good God!вБ†вАФCount Mancini, an anarchist. ThatвАЩs the only thing IвАЩve missed.
He IsnвАЩt there a way of settling it somehow?
Mancini Is there a way of getting money, somehow?
He And the Baron?
Mancini Oh, yes! HeвАЩs just waiting for it, the bloodsucker! HeвАЩll get what heвАЩs after. Some day, youвАЩll see me give him Consuelo for ten thousand francs, perhaps for five!
He Cheap.
Mancini Did I say it was anything else? Do I want to do it? But these bourgeois are strangling me, theyвАЩve got me by the throat. He, one can easily see that youвАЩre a gentleman, and of good society, you understand meвБ†вАФI showed you the jewels which I sent back to himвБ†вАФdamn honestyвБ†вАФI didnвАЩt even dare change the stones, put false onesвБ†вАФ
He Why?
Mancini It would have queered the game. Do you think he didnвАЩt weigh the diamonds when he got them back?
He He will not marry her.
Mancini Yes he will. You donвАЩt understand. Laughs. The first half of his life, this man had only appetitesвБ†вАФnow loveвАЩs got him. If he does not get Consuelo, he is lost, he isвБ†вАФlike a withered narcissus. Plague take him with his automobiles. Did you see his car?
He I did.вБ†вАКвБ†вА¶ Give Consuelo to the JockeyвБ†вАФ
Mancini To Bezano? Laughs. What nonsense you do talk! Oh, I know. ItвАЩs your joke about Adam and Eve. But please stop it. ItвАЩs clever, but it compromises the child. She told me about it.
He Or give her to me.
Mancini Have you a billion? Laughs. Ah, He, IвАЩm not in the proper mood to listen to your clownish jokesвБ†вАФThey say there are terrible jails in this country, and no discriminations are being made between people of my kind, and plain scoundrels. Why do you look at me like that? YouвАЩre making fun of me?
He No.
Mancini IвАЩll never get accustomed to those faces. YouвАЩre so disgustingly made up.
He He will not marry her. You can be as proud as you please, Mancini, but heвАЩll not marry her. What is Consuelo? She is not educated. When she is off her horse, any good housemaid from a decent house has nicer manners, and speaks better. Nonchalantly. DonвАЩt you think sheвАЩs stupid?
Mancini No, sheвАЩs not stupid. And you, He, are a fool. What need has a woman of intelligence? Why, He, you astonish me. Consuelo is an unpolished jewel, and only a real donkey does not notice her sparkle. Do you know what happened? I tried to begin to polish herвБ†вАФ
He Yes, you took a teacher. And what happened?
Mancini Nodding his head. I was frightenedвБ†вАФit went too fastвБ†вАФI had to dismiss him. Another month or two, and she would have kicked me out. Laughs. The clever old diamond merchants of Amsterdam keep their precious stones unpolished, and fool the thieves. My father taught me that.
He The sleep of a diamond. It is only sleeping, then. You are wise, Mancini.
Mancini Do you know what blood flows in the veins of an Italian woman? The blood of Hannibal and CorsiniвБ†вАФof a BorgiaвБ†вАФand of a dirty Lombardi peasantвБ†вАФand of a Moor. Oh! an Italian woman is not of a lower race, with only peasants and gypsies behind her. All possibilities, all forms are included in her, as in our marvelous sculpture. Do you understand that, you fool? Strike hereвБ†вАФout springs a washerwoman, or a cheap street girl whom you want to throw out, because she is sloppy and has a screechy voice. Strike thereвБ†вАФbut carefully and gently, for there stands a queen, a goddess, the Venus of the Capitol, who sings like a Stradivarius and makes you cry, idiot! An Italian womanвБ†вАФ
He YouвАЩre quite a poet, Mancini! But what will the Baron make of her?
Mancini What? What? Make of her? A baroness, you fool! What are you laughing at? I donвАЩt get you? But I am happy that this lovesick beast is neither a duke nor a princeвБ†вАФor she would be a princess and IвБ†вАФwhat would become of me? A year after the wedding they would not let me even into the kitchen laughing not even into the kitchen! I, Count Mancini, and she aвБ†вАФa simpleвБ†вАФ
He Jumping up. What did you say? You are not her father, Mancini?
Mancini TssвБ†вАФthe devilвБ†вАФI am so nervous today! Heavens, who do you think I am? вАЬHer father?вАЭ Of course tries to laugh how silly you areвБ†вАФhavenвАЩt you noticed the family resemblance? Just look, the nose, the eyesвБ†вАФSuddenly sighs deeply. Ah, He! How unhappy I am! Think of it. Here I am, a gentleman, nearly beaten in my struggle to keep up the honour of my name, of an old house, while there in the parquetвБ†вАФthere sits that beast, an elephant with the eyes of a spiderвБ†вАКвБ†вА¶ and he looks at ConsueloвБ†вАКвБ†вА¶ and.вБ†вАКвБ†вА¶
He Yes, yes, he has the motionless stare of a spiderвБ†вАФyouвАЩre right!
Mancini Just what I sayвБ†вАФa spider! But I must, I shall compel him to marry her. YouвАЩll seeвБ†вАФWalking excitedly up and down, playing with his cane. YouвАЩll see! All my life IвАЩve been getting ready for this battle. He continues to walk up and down. Silence. Outside, great stillness.
He Listening. Why is it so quiet out there? What a strange silence.
Mancini Disgusted. I donвАЩt know. Out there it is quietвБ†вАФbut here touching his forehead with his cane here is storm, whirlwind. Bends over the clown. He, shall I tell you a strange thingвБ†вАФan unusual trick of nature? Laughs, and looks very important. For three centuries the Counts Mancini have had no children! Laughs.
He Then how were you born?
Mancini Sh! Silence! That is the secret of our sainted mothers! Ha-ha! We are too ancient a stockвБ†вАФtoo exquisitely refined to trouble ourselves with such thingsвБ†вАФmatters in which a peasant is more competent than ourselves. Enter an usher. What do you want? The manager is on the stage.
The Usher Yes, sir. Baron Regnard wished me to give you this letter.
Mancini The Baron? Is he there?
The Usher Baron Regnard has left. There is no answer.
Mancini Opening the envelope, his hand shaking. The devilвБ†вАФthe devil! The usher is going.
He Just a minute. Why is there no music? This silence.вБ†вАКвБ†вА¶
The Usher It is the act with Madame Zinida and her lions. He goes. Mancini is reading the BaronвАЩs note for the second time.
He WhatвАЩs the matter, Mancini? You shine like JacksonвАЩs sun.
Mancini WhatвАЩs the matter, did you ask? WhatвАЩs the matter? WhatвАЩs the matter? Balancing his cane, he takes steps like a ballet-dancer.
He Mancini! Mancini rolls his eyes, makes faces, dances. Speak, you beast!
Mancini Holds out his hand. Give me ten francs! QuickвБ†вАФten francsвБ†вАФhere, come on. Puts it automatically into his vest pocket. Listen, He! If in a month I donвАЩt have a car of my own, you may give me one of your slaps!
He What! HeвАЩs going to marry? HeвАЩs decided?
Mancini What do you mean by вАЬdecided?вАЭ Laughs. When a man has the rope about his neck, you donвАЩt ask him about his health! BaronвБ†вАФStops suddenly, startled. Briquet is staggering in like a drunken man, his hand over his eyes.
He Goes to him, touches his shoulder gently. What is the matter, Papa Briquet? Tell me!
Briquet Groaning. Oh, oh, I canвАЩtвБ†вАКвБ†вА¶ I canвАЩtвБ†вАКвБ†вА¶ AhвБ†вАФ
He Something has happened? You are ill? Please speak.
Briquet I canвАЩt look at it! Takes his hands from his eyes, opens them wide. Why does she do it? Ah, ah, why does she do it? She must be taken away; she is insane. I couldnвАЩt look at it. Shivers. They will tear her to pieces. HeвБ†вАФher lionsвБ†вАФthey will tear herвБ†вАФ
Mancini Go on, Briquet. She is always like that. You act like a child. You ought to be ashamed.
Briquet NoвБ†вАФToday she is mad! And what is the matter with the crowd? They are all like dead peopleвБ†вАФtheyвАЩre not even breathing. I couldnвАЩt stand it. ListenвБ†вАФwhatвАЩs that? All listen. There is the same silence.
Mancini Disturbed. IвАЩll go and see.
Briquet Yelling. No! DonвАЩt! You canвАЩt lookвБ†вАФdamned profession! DonвАЩt go. You will scorch herвБ†вАФevery pair of eyes that looks at herвБ†вАФat her lionsвБ†вАФno, no. It is impossibleвБ†вАФit is a sacrilege. I ran away.вБ†вАКвБ†вА¶ He, they will tear herвБ†вАФ
He Tries to be cheerful. Keep cool, Papa BriquetвБ†вАФI had no idea you were such a coward. You ought to be ashamed. Have a drink. Mancini, give him some wine.
Briquet I donвАЩt want any. Heavens, if it were only overвБ†вАФAll listen. I have seen many things in my life, but this.вБ†вАКвБ†вА¶ Oh, she is crazy. All still listen. Suddenly the silence breaks, like a huge stone wall crashing. There is a thunder of applause, mixed with shouts, music, wild screamsвБ†вАФhalf bestial, half human. The men give way, relieved. Briquet sinks to a seat.
Mancini Nervous. You seeвБ†вАФyou seeвБ†вАФyou old fool!
Briquet Sobs and laughs. I am not going to allow it any more!
He Here she is!
Zinida walks in, alone. She looks like a drunken bacchante, or like a mad woman. Her hair falls over her shoulders dishevelled, one shoulder is uncovered. She walks unseeing, though her eyes glow. She is like the living statue of a mad Victory. Behind her comes an actor, very pale, then two clowns, and a little later Consuelo and Bezano. All look at Zinida fearfully, as if they were afraid of a touch of her hand, or her great eyes.
Briquet Shouting. You are crazyвБ†вАФyouвАЩre a mad woman!
Zinida I? No. Did you see? Did you see? Well? She stands smiling, with the expression of a mad Victory.
Tilly Plaintively. Cut it out, Zinida. Go to the devil!
Zinida You saw, too! And!вБ†вАКвБ†вА¶ whatвБ†вАФ
Briquet Come homeвБ†вАФcome home. To the others. You can do what you like here. Zinida, come home.
Polly You canвАЩt go, Papa. ThereвАЩs still your number.
Zinida Her eyes meet those of Bezano. Ah! Bezano. Laughs long and happily. Bezano! Alfred! Did you see? My lions do love me! Bezano, without answering, leaves the stage. Zinida seems to wither and grow dim, as a light being extinguished. Her smile fades, her eyes and face grow pale. Briquet anxiously bends over her.
Briquet In a slow voice. A chair! Zinida sits. Her head drops on her shoulder, her arms fall, she begins to shiver and tremble. Someone calls, вАЬCognacвАЭвБ†вАФan actor runs to get it.
Briquet Helpless. What is the matter, Zinida darling?
Mancini Running about. She must quiet down. Get out, get outвБ†вАФvagabonds! IвАЩll fix everything, Papa Briquet. The wrapвБ†вАФwhereвАЩs the wrap? SheвАЩs cold. A clown hands it to him; they cover her.
Tilly Timidly. WouldnвАЩt you like some moosic?
Mancini Giving her some cognac. Drink, Duchess, drink! Drink it allвБ†вАФthatвАЩs it. Zinida drinks it like water, evidently not noticing the taste. She shivers. The clowns disappear one by one. Consuelo, with a sudden flexible movement, falls on her knees before Zinida and kisses her hands, warming them between her own.
Consuelo Dear, dear, you are cold! Poor little hands, dear good one, beloved oneвБ†вАФ
Zinida Pushes her away, gently. HoвБ†вАФhome. It will soon be over. ItвАЩs nothingвБ†вАКвБ†вА¶ I am verвБ†вАФveryвБ†вАКвБ†вА¶ home.вБ†вАКвБ†вА¶ You stay here, BriquetвБ†вАФyou must. IвАЩm all right.
Consuelo You are cold? Here is my shawl.
Zinida NoвБ†вАФlet me.вБ†вАКвБ†вА¶ Consuelo gets up, and moves aside.
Briquet And itвАЩs all because of your books, ZinidaвБ†вАФyour mythology. Now tell me, why do you want those beasts to love you? Beasts! Do you understand, He? You too, youвАЩre from that world. SheвАЩll listen more to you. Explain it to her. Whom can those beasts love? Those hairy monsters, with diabolic eyes?
He Genially. I believeвБ†вАФonly their equals. You are right, Papa BriquetвБ†вАФthere must be the same race.
Briquet Of course, and this is all nonsenseвБ†вАФliterature. Explain it to her, He.
He Takes on a meditative air. Yes, you are right, Briquet.
Briquet You see, dear, silly womanвБ†вАФeverybody agrees.вБ†вАКвБ†вА¶
Mancini Oh! Briquet, you make me sick; you are an absolute despot, an Asiatic.
Zinida With the shadow of a smile, gives her hand to be kissed. Calm yourself, Louis. It is overвБ†вАФI am going home. She stands up, shaking, still chilled.
Briquet But how? alone, dear?
Mancini What! fool! Did you imagine that Count Mancini would leave a woman when she needed help? I shall take her homeвБ†вАФlet your brutal heart be at restвБ†вАФI shall take her home. Thomas, run for an automobile. DonвАЩt push me Briquet, you are as awkward as a unicornвБ†вАКвБ†вА¶ thatвАЩs the way, thatвАЩs the wayвБ†вАФThey are holding her, guiding her slowly toward the door. Consuelo, her chin resting in her hand, is following them with her eyes. Unconsciously she assumes a somewhat affected pose.
Mancini IвАЩll come back for you, childвБ†вАФOnly He and Consuelo are left on the stage. In the ring, music, shrieks, and laughter begin again.
He ConsueloвБ†вАФ
Consuelo Is that you, He, dear?
He Where did you learn that pose? I have seen it only in marble. You look like Psyche.
Consuelo I donвАЩt know, He. She sighs and sits on the sofa, keeping in her pose the same artificiality and beauty. ItвАЩs all so sad here, today. He, are you sorry for Zinida?
He What did she do?
Consuelo I didnвАЩt see. I had closed my eyes, and didnвАЩt open them. Alfred says she is a wicked woman, but that isnвАЩt true. She has such nice eyes, and what tiny cold handsвБ†вАФas if she were dead. What does she do it for? Alfred says she should be audacious, beautiful, but quiet, otherwise what she does is only disgusting. It isnвАЩt true, is it, He?
He She loves Alfred.
Consuelo Alfred? My Bezano? Shrugging her shoulders, and surprised. How does she love him? The same as everyone loves?
He YesвБ†вАФas everyone lovesвБ†вАФor still more.
Consuelo Bezano? Bezano? NoвБ†вАФitвАЩs nonsense. Pause; silence. What a beautiful costume you have, He. You invented it yourself?
He Jim helped me.
Consuelo Jim is so nice! All clowns are nice.
He I am wicked.
Consuelo Laughs. You? You are the nicest of all. Oh, goodness! Three acts more! This is the second on now. Alfred and I are in the third. Are you coming to see me?
He I always do. How beautiful you are, Consuelo.
Consuelo Like Eve? Smiles.
He Yes, Consuelo. And if the Baron asks you to be his wife, will you accept?
Consuelo Certainly, He. ThatвАЩs all Father and I are waiting for. Father told me yesterday that the Baron will not hesitate very long. Of course I do not love him. But I will be his honest, faithful wife. Father wants to teach me to play the piano.
He Are those your own wordsвБ†вАФвАЬhis honest, faithful wifeвАЭ?
Consuelo Certainly they are mine. Whose could they be? He loves me so much, the poor thing. Dear He, what does вАЬloveвАЭ mean? Everybody speaks of loveвБ†вАФloveвБ†вАФZinida, too! Poor Zinida! What a boring evening this has been! He, did you paint the laughter on your face yourself?
He My own self, dear little ConsueloвБ†вАФ
Consuelo How do you do it, all of you? I tried once, but couldnвАЩt do a thing. Why are there no women clowns? Why are you so silent, He? You, too, are sad, tonight.
He No, I am happy tonight. Give me your hand, Consuelo, I want to see what it says.
Consuelo Do you know how? What a talented man you are! Read it, but donвАЩt lie, like a gypsy. He goes down on one knee and takes her hand. Both bend over it. Am I lucky?
He Yes, lucky. But wait a minuteвБ†вАФthis line hereвБ†вАФfunny. Ah, Consuelo, what does it say, here! Acting. I tremble, my eyes do not dare to read the strange, fatal signs. ConsueloвБ†вАФ
Consuelo The stars are talking.
He Yes, the stars are talking. Their voices are distant and terrible; their rays are pale, and their shadows slip by, like the ghosts of dead virginsвБ†вАФtheir spell is upon thee, Consuelo, beautiful Consuelo. Thou standest at the door of Eternity.
Consuelo I donвАЩt understand. Does it mean that I will live long?
He This lineвБ†вАФhow far it goes. Strange! Thou wilt live eternally, Consuelo.
Consuelo You see, He, you did tell me a lie, just like a gypsy!
He But it is writtenвБ†вАФhere, sillyвБ†вАФand here. Now think of what the stars are saying. Here you have eternal life, love, and glory; and here, listen to what Jupiter says. He says: вАЬGoddess, thou must not belong to anyone born on earth,вАЭ and if you marry the BaronвБ†вАФyouвАЩll perish, youвАЩll die, Consuelo. Consuelo laughs.
Consuelo Will he eat me?
He No. But you will die before he has time to eat you.
Consuelo And what will become of Father? Is there nothing about him here? Laughing, she softly sings the melody of the waltz, which is playing in the distance.
He DonвАЩt laugh, Consuelo, at the voice of the stars. They are far away, their rays are light and pale, and we can barely see their sleeping shadows, but their sorcery is stern and dark. You stand at the gates of eternity. Your die is cast; you are doomedвБ†вАФand your Alfred, whom you love in your heart, even though your mind is not aware of it, your Alfred cannot save you. He, too, is a stranger on this earth. He is submerged in a deep sleep. He, too, is a little god who has lost himself, and Consuelo, never, never will he find his way to Heaven again. Forget BezanoвБ†вАФ
Consuelo I donвАЩt understand a word. Do the gods really exist? My teacher told me about them. But I thought it was all tales! Laughs. And my Bezano is a god?
He Forget Bezano! Consuelo, do you know who can save you? The only one who can save you? I.
Consuelo Laughing. You, He?
He Yes, but donвАЩt laugh! Look. Here is the letter H. It is I, He.
Consuelo He Who Gets Slapped? Is that written here, too?
He That, too. The stars know everything. But look here, what more is written about him. Consuelo, welcome him. He is an old god in disguise, who came down to earth only to love you, foolish little Consuelo.
Consuelo Laughing and singing. Some god!
He DonвАЩt mock! The gods donвАЩt like such, empty laughter from beautiful lips. The gods grow lonely and die, when they are not recognized. Oh, Consuelo! Oh, great joy and love! Do recognize this god, and accept him. Think a moment, one day a god suddenly went crazy!
Consuelo Gods go crazy, too?
He Yes, when they are half man, then they often go mad. Suddenly he saw his own sublimity, and shuddered with horror, with infinite solitude, with superhuman anguish. It is terrible, when anguish touches the divine soul!
Consuelo I donвАЩt like it. What language are you speaking? I donвАЩt understandвБ†вАФ
He I speak the language of thy awakening. Consuelo, recognize and accept thy god, who was thrown down from the summit like a stone. Accept the god who fell to the earth in order to live, to play, and to be infinitely drunk with joy. Evo√Ђ Goddess!
Consuelo Tortured. HeвБ†вАФI cannot understand. Let my hand alone.
He Stands up. Sleep. Then wake again, Consuelo! And when thou wakestвБ†вАФremember that hour when, covered with snow-white sea-foam, thou didst emerge from the sky-blue waters. Remember heaven, and the slow eastern wind, and the whisper of the foam at thy marble feet.
Consuelo Her eyes are closed. I believeвБ†вАФwaitвБ†вАФI remember. Remind me furtherвБ†вАФ
He is bowed over Consuelo, with lifted arms; he speaks slowly, but in a commanding voice, as if conjuring.
He You see the waves playing. Remember the song of the sirens, their sorrowless song of joy. Their white bodies, shining blue through the blue waters. Or can you hear the sun, singing? Like the strings of a divine harp, spread the golden raysвБ†вАФDo you not see the hand of God, which gives harmony, light, and love to the world? Do not the mountains, in the blue cloud of incense, sing their hymn of glory? Remember, O Consuelo, remember the prayer of the mountains, the prayer of the sea. Silence.
He Commandingly. RememberвБ†вАФConsuelo!
Consuelo Opening her eyes. No! He, I was feeling so happy, and suddenly I forgot it all. Yet something of it all is still in my heart. Help me again, He, remind me. It hurts, I hear so many voices. They all sing вАЬConsueloвБ†вАФConsuelo.вАЭ What comes after? Silence; pause. What comes after? It hurts. Remind me, He. SilenceвБ†вАФin the ring, the music suddenly bursts forth in a tempestuous circus gallop. Silence. He, opens her eyes and smiles thatвАЩs Alfred galloping. Do you recognize his music?
He With rage. Leave the boy alone! Suddenly falls on his knees before Consuelo. I love you, Consuelo, revelation of my heart, light of my nights, I love you, Consuelo. Looks at her in ecstasy and tearsвБ†вАФand gets a slap; starting back. WhatвАЩs this?
Consuelo A slap! You forget who you are. Stands up, with anger in her eyes. You are He Who Gets Slapped! Did you forget it? Some god! With such a faceвБ†вАФslapped face! Was it with slaps they threw you down from heaven, god?
He Wait! DonвАЩt stand up! IвБ†вАФdid not finish the play!
Consuelo Sits. Then you were playing?
He Wait! One minute.
Consuelo You lied to me. Why did you play so that I believed you?
He I am He Who Gets Slapped!
Consuelo You are not angry because I struck you? I did not want to really, but you were soвБ†вАФdisgusting. And now you are so funny again. You have great talent, HeвБ†вАФor are you drunk?
He Strike me again.
Consuelo No.
He I need it for my play. Strike!
Consuelo Laughs, and touches his cheek with her fingertips. Here, then!
He DidnвАЩt you understand that you are a queen, and I a fool who is in love with his queen? DonвАЩt you know, Consuelo, that every queen has a fool, and he is always in love with her, and they always beat him for it. He Who Gets Slapped.
Consuelo No. I didnвАЩt know.
He Yes, every queen. Beauty has her fool. Wisdom, too. Oh, how many fools she has! Her court is overcrowded with enamoured fools, and the sound of slaps does not cease, even through the night. But I never received such a sweet slap as the one given by my little queen. Someone appears at the door. He notices it, and continues to play, making many faces. Clown He can have no rival! Who is there who could stand such a deluge of slaps, such a hailstorm of slaps, and not get soaked? Feigns to cry aloud. вАЬHave pity on me. I am but a poor fool!вАЭ
Enter two men: an actor, dressed as a bareback rider, and a gentleman from the audience. He is spare, dressed in black, very respectable. He carries his hat in his hand.
Consuelo Laughing, embarrassed. He, there is someone here. Stop!
He Gets up. Who is it? Who dares to intrude in the castle of my queen?
He stops, suddenly. Consuelo, laughing, jumps up and runs away, after a quick glance at the gentleman.
Consuelo You cheered me up, He. Goodbye. At the door. You shall get a note tomorrow.
The Bareback Rider Laughing. A jolly fellow, sir. You wanted to see him? There he is. He, the gentleman wants to see you.
He In a depressed voice. What can I do for you?
The actor bows, and goes away, smiling. Both men take a step toward each other.
Gentleman Is this you?
He Yes! It is I. And you? Silence.
Gentleman Must I believe my eyes? Is this you, Mr.вБ†вАФ
He In a rage. My name here is He. I have no other name, do you hear? He Who Gets Slapped. And if you want to stay here, donвАЩt forget it.
Gentleman You are so familiar. As far as I can rememberвБ†вАФ
He We are all familiar, here. Contemptuously. Besides, thatвАЩs all you deserve, anywhere.
Gentleman Humbly. You have not forgiven me, He? Silence.
He Are you here with my wife? Is she, too, in the circus?
Gentleman Quickly. Oh, no! I am alone. She stayed there!
He YouвАЩve left her already?
Gentleman Humbly. NoвБ†вАФwe haveвБ†вАФa son. After your sudden and mysterious disappearanceвБ†вАФwhen you left that strange and insulting letterвБ†вАФ
He Laughs. Insulting? You are still able to feel insults? What are you doing here? Were you looking for me, or is it an accident?
Gentleman I have been looking for you, for half a yearвБ†вАФthrough many countries. And suddenly, todayвБ†вАФby accident, indeedвБ†вАФI had no acquaintances here, and I went to the circus. We must talk things overвБ†вАКвБ†вА¶ He, I implore you. Silence.
He Here is a shadow I cannot lose! To talk things over! Do you really think we still have something to talk over? All right. Leave your address with the porter, and I will let you know when you can see me. Now get out. Proudly. I am busy.
The gentleman bows and leaves. He does not return his bow, but stands with outstretched hand, in the pose of a great man, who shows a boring visitor the door.
Curtain.
